steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The sentence says the two broods “coincided” — they arrived at the same time. The blank must name this rare event of two separate cycles meeting at one moment, something that “happens only once every 221 years.”

steps.title: Test each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — a succession: a succession means one thing following another in sequence — that implies one brood came after the other, not simultaneously. ✗
  • Choice B — a division: a division means a splitting apart — the opposite of what happened; the two broods came together, not apart. ✗
  • Choice C — a convergence: a convergence means separate things coming together at one point — exactly describes two broods with different cycles meeting simultaneously. ✓
  • Choice D — an expiration: an expiration means an ending or dying out — contradicts the passage, which describes the cicadas emerging and covering sixteen states. ✗
That matches Choice C — a convergence.
